readiness_rows.reading: The information registry now has enough structure to support deep review and cycle reconstruction without flattening layers.
The adjacent pool is no longer flat; several rows are preserved cleanly while unresolved rows remain explicit pending assets.
The chronology layer now distinguishes event, lag, and quiet windows well enough for bounded cycle reconstruction.
Mixed-relevance rows are preserved with explicit buckets, which is useful for review and later factor discovery but not for immediate training.

remaining_gap_rows.why_it_still_matters: Several adjacent rows remain structurally mixed, which weakens direct training use even though research review can proceed.
Chronology grammar exists, but daily concept-index, breadth, and turnover sequences are not yet normalized into a full day-by-day table.
Public catalyst anchors exist, but the forward calendar is not yet converted into a research-usable recurring table.
The noisy rows are preserved, but they have not yet been tested as A-share-specific spillover factors or excluded from that path.

interpretation: The CPO information foundation has crossed the threshold for bounded deep research.
It has not crossed the threshold for formal training because several material gaps remain unresolved.
The correct next move is an owner-level decision on the next research layer, not automatic training or feature promotion.
